description_long: This is a classic propeller-driven passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a polished metallic finish, bearing the livery of Iberia Líneas Aéreas Españolas. The aircraft features four large radial engines mounted on a straight, low-set wing, each with a three-bladed propeller. The tail section is distinctive, with a triple vertical stabilizer configuration and a horizontal stabilizer connecting them. The landing gear is visible and consists of a tricycle arrangement with two main wheels and a nose wheel. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the flight crew. The exterior is primarily white and silver, accented with red and yellow stripes and branding.
description: A vintage Iberia passenger airplane with four propeller engines, triple tail fins, and a polished metallic fuselage with red and yellow accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing the four propeller engines, straight wings, and the nose of the airplane.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the Iberia branding, passenger windows, and the triple tail fin configuration.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the triple vertical stabilizers, horizontal stabilizer, and the main landing gear.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the smooth fuselage, engine placement, and the distinctive tail design.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with slight dihedral and no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Four radial piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a three-bladed propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Triple vertical stabilizer with a conventional horizontal stabilizer, forming a distinctive H-tail configuration.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose wheel, all appearing retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
